Subparagraph.Text: is paid or payable for food or beverages provided at a construction work camp, at which the taxpayer is lodged, that was constructed or installed at or near the site to provide board and lodging to employees while they are engaged in construction services at the site; or

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to amounts paid or payable in respect of food and beverages provided after 2001.
The portion of subsection 70(9) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Transfer of farm property to child
Subsection.Text: If any land in Canada or depreciable property in Canada of a prescribed class of a taxpayer to which subsection (5) would otherwise apply was, before the taxpayer’s death, used principally in a farming business in which the taxpayer, the taxpayer’s spouse or common-law partner or any of the taxpayer’s children was actively engaged on a regular and continuous basis (or, in the case of property used in the operation of a woodlot, was engaged to the extent required by a prescribed forest management plan in respect of that woodlot), the property is, as a consequence of the death, transferred or distributed to a child of the taxpayer who was resident in Canada immediately before the death and it can be shown, within the period ending 36 months after the death or, if written application that this subsection apply has been made to the Minister by the taxpayer’s legal representative within that period, within any longer period that the Minister considers reasonable in the circumstances, that the property has vested indefeasibly in the child,
Subparagraph 70(9.3)(b)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a share in the capital stock of a Canadian corporation that would be a share in the capital stock of a family farm corporation if paragraph (a) of the definition share of the capital stock of a family farm corporation in subsection (10) were read without the words “in which the person or a spouse, common-law partner, child or parent of the person was actively engaged on a regular and continuous basis (or, in the case of property used in the operation of a woodlot, was engaged to the extent required by a prescribed forest management plan in respect of that woodlot)”, or

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph (a) of the definition interest in a family farm partnership in subsection 70(10) of the Act after subparagraph (iv) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedParagraph.Text: principally in the course of carrying on a farming business in Canada in which the person or a spouse, common-law partner, child or parent of the person was actively engaged on a regular and continuous basis (or, in the case of property used in the operation of a woodlot, was engaged to the extent required by a prescribed forest management plan in respect of that woodlot),

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph (a) of the definition share of the capital stock of a family farm corporation in subsection 70(10) of the Act after subparagraph (iv) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedParagraph.Text: principally in the course of carrying on a farming business in Canada in which the person or a spouse, common-law partner, child or parent of the person was actively engaged on a regular and continuous basis (or, in the case of property used in the operation of a woodlot, was engaged to the extent required by a prescribed forest management plan in respect of that woodlot),

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to transfers of property that occur as a consequence of deaths that occur after December 10, 2001.
Subsection (2) applies to transfers and distributions of property that occur after December 10, 2001.
Subsections (3) and (4) apply to transfers of property that occur after December 10, 2001.
The portion of subsection 73(3) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Inter vivos transfer of farm property to child
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Part, if at any time any land in Canada or depreciable property in Canada of a prescribed class of a taxpayer or any eligible capital property in respect of a business carried on in Canada by a taxpayer is transferred by the taxpayer to a child of the taxpayer who was resident in Canada immediately before the transfer, and the property was, before the transfer, used principally in a farming business in which the taxpayer, the taxpayer’s spouse or common-law partner or any of the taxpayer’s children was actively engaged on a regular and continuous basis (or, in the case of property used in the operation of a woodlot, was engaged to the extent required by a prescribed forest management plan in respect of that woodlot),
Subsection (1) applies to transfers of property that occur after December 10, 2001.
Section 85 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (5):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Acquisition of apprentice tools, re capital cost and deemed depreciation
Subsection.Text: If subsection (1) has applied in respect of the acquisition at any particular time of any depreciable property by a corporation from an individual, the cost of the property to the individual was included in computing an amount under paragraph 8(1)(r) in respect of the individual, and the amount that would be the cost of the property to the individual immediately before the transfer if this Act were read without reference to subsection 8(7) (which amount is in this subsection referred to as the “individual’s original cost”) exceeds the individual’s proceeds of disposition of the property,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the capital cost to the corporation of the property is deemed to be equal to the individual’s original cost; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount by which the individual’s original cost exceeds the individual’s proceeds of disposition in respect of the property is deemed to have been deducted by the corporation under paragraph 20(1)(a) in respect of the property in computing income for taxation years that ended before that particular time.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to dispositions that occur after 2001.
Paragraph 87(2)(j.92) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (j.92)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of subsection 125(5.1) and the definition eligible corporation in subsection 157.1(1), the new corporation is deemed to be the same corporation as, and a continuation of, each predecessor corporation;

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 87(2)(oo.1) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.MarginalNote: Refundable investment tax credit and balance-due day
Paragraph.Label: (oo.1)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of applying the definition qualifying corporation in subsection 127.1(2), and subparagraph (d)(i) of the definition balance-due day in subsection 248(1), to any corporation, the new corporation is deemed to have had

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) apply to taxation years that end after 2001.
The portion of paragraph 88(1)(e.9) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e.9)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of applying the definition qualifying corporation in subsection 127.1(2), and subparagraph (d)(i) of the definition balance-due day in subsection 248(1), to any corporation (other than the subsidiary)

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that end after 2001.
Section 97 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Acquisition of apprentice tools, re capital cost and deemed depreciation
Subsection.Text: If subsection (2) has applied in respect of the acquisition at any particular time of any depreciable property by a partnership from an individual, the cost of the property to the individual was included in computing an amount under paragraph 8(1)(r) in respect of the individual, and the amount that would be the cost of the property to the individual immediately before the transfer if this Act were read without reference to subsection 8(7) (which amount is in this subsection referred to as the “individual’s original cost”) exceeds the individual’s proceeds of disposition of the property,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the capital cost to the partnership of the property is deemed to be equal to the individual’s original cost; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount by which the individual’s original cost exceeds the individual’s proceeds of disposition in respect of the property is deemed to have been deducted by the partnership under paragraph 20(1)(a) in respect of the property in computing income for taxation years that ended before that particular time.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to dispositions that occur after 2001.
Subparagraph 110(1)(d.01)(ii) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 110(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (f):

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: any amount that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is received by the taxpayer in the year under a program referred to in subparagraph 56(1)(r)(ii) or (iii), a program established under the authority of the Department of Human Resources Development Act or a prescribed program,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is financial assistance for the payment of tuition fees of the taxpayer that are not included in computing an amount deductible under subsection 118.5(1) in computing the taxpayer’s tax payable under this Part for any taxation year,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: is included in computing the taxpayer’s income for the year, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: is not otherwise deductible in computing the taxpayer’s taxable income for the year;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to dispositions that occur after 2001.

Paragraph 110(1)(g) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (2), applies to the 1997 and subsequent taxation years and, notwithstanding subsections 152(4) to (5) of the Act, any assessment of a taxpayer’s tax, interest or penalty for any taxation year shall be made that is necessary to give effect to that paragraph.
The description of E in the definition non-capital loss in subsection 111(8) of the Act is replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is the taxpayer’s loss for the year from an office, employment, business or property, the taxpayer’s allowable business investment loss for the year, an amount deducted under paragraph (1)(b) or section 110.6 in computing the taxpayer’s taxable income for the year or an amount that may be deducted under any of paragraphs 110(1)(d) to (d.3), (f), (g), (j) and (k), section 112 and subsections 113(1) and 138(6) in computing the taxpayer’s taxable income for the year, and

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 1997 and subsequent taxation years and, notwithstanding subsections 152(4) to (5) of the Act, any assessment of a taxpayer’s tax, interest or penalty for any taxation year shall be made that is necessary to give effect to subsection (1).
The definition qualified non-resident in subsection 115.2(1) of the Act is repealed.
The definitions designated investment services and promoter in subsection 115.2(1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: designated investment services provided to a person or partnership means any one or more of the services described in the following paragraphs:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: investment management and advice with respect to qualified investments, regardless of whether the manager has discretionary authority to buy or sell;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: purchasing and selling qualified investments, exercising rights incidental to the ownership of qualified investments such as voting, conversion and exchange, and entering into and executing agreements with respect to such purchasing and selling and the exercising of such rights;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: investment administration services, such as receiving, delivering and having custody of investments, calculating and reporting investment values, receiving subscription amounts from, and paying distributions and proceeds of disposition to, investors in and beneficiaries of the person or partnership, record keeping, accounting and reporting to the person or partnership and its investors and beneficiaries; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a corporation, trust or partnership the only undertaking of which is the investing of its funds in qualified investments, marketing investments in the corporation, trust or partnership to non-resident investors.

Definition.Text: promoter of a corporation, trust or partnership means a particular person or partnership that initiates or directs the founding, organization or substantial reorganization of the corporation, trust or partnership, and a person or partnership that is affiliated with the particular person or partnership.

Subsection.Text: The portion of the definition qualified investment in subsection 115.2(1) of the Act before subparagraph (a)(ii) is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: qualified investment of a person or partnership means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a share of the capital stock of a corporation, or an interest in a partnership, trust, entity, fund or organization, other than a share or an interest

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: that is either

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: not listed on a prescribed stock exchange, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: listed on a prescribed stock exchange, if the person or partnership, together with all persons with whom the person or partnership does not deal at arm’s length, owns 25% or more of the issued shares of any class of the capital stock of the corporation or of the total value of interests in the partnership, entity, trust, fund or organization, as the case may be, and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 115.2(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: Canadian investor, at any time in respect of a non-resident person, means a person that the non-resident person knows, or ought to know after reasonable inquiry, is at that time resident in Canada.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 115.2(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Not carrying on business in Canada
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection 115(1) and Part XIV, a non-resident person is not considered to be carrying on business in Canada at any particular time solely because of the provision to the person, or to a partnership of which the person is a member, at the particular time of designated investment services by a Canadian service provider if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of services provided to a non-resident individual other than a trust, the individual is not affiliated at the particular time with the Canadian service provider;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of services provided to a non-resident person that is a corporation or trust,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the person has not, before the particular time, directly or through its agents,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: directed any promotion of investments in itself principally at Canadian investors, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: sold an investment in itself that is outstanding at the particular time to a person who was a Canadian investor at the time of the sale and who is a Canadian investor at the particular time,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the person has not, before the particular time, directly or through its agents, filed any document with a public authority in Canada in accordance with the securities legislation of Canada or of any province in order to permit the distribution of interests in the person to persons resident in Canada, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: when the particular time is more than one year after the time at which the person was created, the total of the fair market value, at the particular time, of investments in the person that are beneficially owned by persons and partnerships (other than a designated entity in respect of the Canadian service provider) that are affiliated with the Canadian service provider does not exceed 25% of the fair market value, at the particular time, of all investments in the person; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of services provided to a partnership of which the non-resident person is a member,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the particular time is not more than one year after the partnership was formed, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of the fair market value, at the particular time, of investments in the partnership that are beneficially owned by persons and partnerships (other than a designated entity in respect of the Canadian service provider) that are affiliated with the Canadian service provider does not exceed 25% of the fair market value, at the particular time, of all investments in the partnership.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 115.2(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purpose of section 247, where subsection (2) applies in respect of services provided to a person that is a corporation or trust or to a partnership, if the Canadian service provider referred to in that subsection does not deal at arm’s length with the promoter of the person or of the partnership, the service provider is deemed not to deal at arm’s length with the person or partnership.
Subsections (1) to (3) and (5) and (6) apply to the 2002 and subsequent taxation years.

Subsection (4) applies to the 1999 and subsequent taxation years except that, in applying the definition Canadian investor, as enacted by that subsection, to taxation years that end after 1998 and before 2002, that definition shall be read as follows:

Definition.Text: Canadian investor, at any time in respect of a qualified non-resident, means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a person that the non-resident knows, or ought to know after reasonable inquiry, is at that time resident in Canada; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a partnership that the non-resident knows, or ought to know after reasonable inquiry, has a member that is at that time resident in Canada.

Subsection.Text: In applying subparagraph 115.2(2)(b)(i) of the Act, as enacted by subsection 21(1) of the Income Tax Amendments Act, 1999, chapter 19 of the Statutes of Canada, 2000, to taxation years that end after 1998 and before 2002, that subparagraph shall be read as follows:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the non-resident has not, before the particular time, directly or through its agents,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: directed any promotion of investments in itself principally at Canadian investors, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: sold an investment in itself that is outstanding at the particular time to a person who was a Canadian investor at the time of the sale and who is a Canadian investor at the particular time,

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition qualifying educational program in subsection 118.6(1) of the Act is amended by striking out the word “or” at the end of subparagraph (i) and by adding the following after subparagraph (ii):

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: an amount that is received by the student in the year under a program referred to in subparagraph 56(1)(r)(ii) or (iii), a program established under the authority of the Department of Human Resources Development Act or a prescribed program, or

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2002 and subsequent taxation years.
Subparagraph 122.3(1)(e)(iii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an amount deducted under section 110.6 or paragraph 111(1)(b), or deductible under paragraph 110(1)(d.2), (d.3), (f), (g) or (j), in computing the individual’s taxable income for the year.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 1997 and subsequent taxation years except that, for the 1997 taxation year, subparagraph 122.3(1)(e)(iii) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), shall be read as follows:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an amount deducted by the individual under section 110.6 or paragraph 111(1)(b), or deductible by the individual under paragraph 110(1)(d.2), (d.3), (f), (g) or (j), for the year or in respect of the period or periods referred to in subparagraph (ii), as the case may be.

Subsection.Text: Notwithstanding subsections 152(4) to (5) of the Act, any assessment of a taxpayer’s tax, interest or penalty for any taxation year shall be made that is necessary to give effect to subsection (1) or (2).
Subsections 122.5(1) to (3.1) of the Act are replaced by the following:
The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: adjusted income of an individual, for a taxation year in relation to a month specified for the taxation year, means the total of the individual’s income for the taxation year and the income for the taxation year of the individual’s qualified relation, if any, in relation to the specified month, both calculated as if no amount were included in respect of any gain from a disposition of property to which section 79 applies.

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: époux ou conjoint de fait visé

Definition.Text: cohabiting spouse or common-law partner of an individual at any time has the meaning assigned by section 122.6.
eligible individual, in relation to a month specified for a taxation year, means an individual (other than a trust) who

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: has, before the specified month, attained the age of 19 years; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: was, at any time before the specified month,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a parent who resided with their child, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: married or in a common-law partnership.

Definition.Text: qualified dependant of an individual, in relation to a month specified for a taxation year, means a person who at the beginning of the specified month

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is the individual’s child or is dependent for support on the individual or on the individual’s cohabiting spouse or common-law partner;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: resides with the individual;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is under the age of 19 years;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: is not an eligible individual in relation to the specified month; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: is not a qualified relation of any individual in relation to the specified month.

Definition.Text: qualified relation of an individual, in relation to a month specified for a taxation year, means the person, if any, who, at the beginning of the specified month, is the individual’s cohabiting spouse or common-law partner.
return of income, in respect of a person for a taxation year, means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for a person who is resident in Canada at the end of the taxation year, the person’s return of income (other than a return of income under subsection 70(2) or 104(23), paragraph 128(2)(e) or subsection 150(4)) that is required to be filed for the taxation year or that would be required to be filed if the person had tax payable under this Part for the taxation year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, a prescribed form containing prescribed information that is filed for the taxation year with the Minister.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Persons not eligible individuals, qualified relations or qualified dependants
Subsection.Text: Notwithstanding subsection (1), a person is not an eligible individual, is not a qualified relation and is not a qualified dependant, in relation to a month specified for a taxation year, if the person

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: died before the specified month;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is confined to a prison or similar institution for a period of at least 90 days that includes the first day of the specified month;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is at the beginning of the specified month a non-resident person, other than a non-resident person who

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is at that time the cohabiting spouse or common-law partner of a person who is deemed under subsection 250(1) to be resident in Canada throughout the taxation year that includes the first day of the specified month, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: was resident in Canada at any time before the specified month;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: is at the beginning of the specified month a person described in paragraph 149(1)(a) or (b); or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: is a person in respect of whom a special allowance under the Children’s Special Allowances Act is payable for the specified month.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Deemed payment on account of tax
Subsection.Text: An eligible individual in relation to a month specified for a taxation year who files a return of income for the taxation year and applies for an amount under this subsection is deemed to have paid during the specified month on account of their tax payable under this Part for the taxation year an amount equal to 1/4 of the amount, if any, determined by the formula

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: $213 for the qualified relation, if any, of the individual in relation to the specified month,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (c)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the individual has no qualified relation in relation to the specified month and is entitled to deduct an amount for the taxation year under subsection 118(1) because of paragraph (b) of the description of B in that subsection in respect of a qualified dependant of the individual in relation to the specified month, $213,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (d)
FormulaParagraph.Text: $112 times the number of qualified dependants of the individual in relation to the specified month, other than a qualified dependant in respect of whom an amount is included under paragraph (c) in computing the total for the specified month,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (e)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the individual has no qualified relation and has one or more qualified dependants, in relation to the specified month, $112, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (f)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the individual has no qualified relation and no qualified dependant, in relation to the specified month, the lesser of $112 and 2% of the amount, if any, by which the individual’s income for the taxation year exceeds $6,911; and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is 5% of the amount, if any, by which the individual’s adjusted income for the taxation year in relation to the specified month exceeds $27,749.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (3.2) applies in respect of an eligible individual in relation to a particular month specified for a taxation year, and each subsequent month specified for the taxation year, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount deemed by that subsection to have been paid by the eligible individual during the particular month specified for the taxation year is less than $25; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it is reasonable to conclude that the amount deemed by that subsection to have been paid by the eligible individual during each subsequent month specified for the taxation year will be less than $25.

Subsection.Text: If this subsection applies, the total of the amounts that would otherwise be deemed by subsection (3) to have been paid on account of the eligible individual’s tax payable under this Part for the taxation year during the particular month specified for the taxation year, and during each subsequent month specified for the taxation year, is deemed to have been paid by the eligible individual on account of their tax payable under this Part for the taxation year during the particular specified month for the taxation year, and the amount deemed by subsection (3) to have been paid by the eligible individual during those subsequent months specified for the taxation year is deemed, except for the purpose of this subsection, not to have been paid to the extent that it is included in an amount deemed to have been paid by this subsection.
Subsections 122.5(5) to (6) of the Act are replaced by the following:

If an individual is a qualified relation of another individual, in relation to a month specified for a taxation year, only one of them is an eligible individual in relation to that specified month, and if both of them claim to be eligible individuals, the individual that the Minister designates is the eligible individual in relation to that specified month.
If a person would, if this Act were read without reference to this subsection, be the qualified dependant of two or more individuals, in relation to a month specified for a taxation year,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person is deemed to be a qualified dependant, in relation to that month, of the one of those individuals on whom those individuals agree;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the absence of an agreement referred to in paragraph (a), the person is deemed to be, in relation to that month, a qualified dependant of the individual, if any, who is, at the beginning of that month, an eligible individual within the meaning assigned by section 122.6 in respect of the person; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, the person is deemed to be, in relation to that month, a qualified dependant only of the individual that the Minister designates.

Subsection.Text: An individual shall notify the Minister of the occurrence of any of the following events before the end of the month following the month in which the event occurs:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the individual ceases to be an eligible individual;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a person becomes or ceases to be the individual’s qualified relation; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a person ceases to be a qualified dependant of the individual, otherwise than because of attaining the age of 19 years.

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of this section, the income of a person who is non-resident at any time in a taxation year is deemed to be equal to the amount that would, if the person were resident in Canada throughout the year, be the person’s income for the year.
Subsections (1) and (2) apply to amounts that are deemed to be paid during months specified for the 2001 and subsequent taxation years.
Subclause 126(1)(b)(ii)(A)(III) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subclause.Label: (III)
Subclause.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an amount deducted under section 110.6 or paragraph 111(1)(b), or deductible under any of paragraphs 110(1)(d) to (d.3), (f), (g) and (j) and sections 112 and 113, in computing the taxpayer’s taxable income for the year, and

Subsection.Text: Subclause 126(2.1)(a)(ii)(A)(III) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subclause.Label: (III)
Subclause.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an amount deducted under section 110.6 or paragraph 111(1)(b), or deductible under any of paragraphs 110(1)(d) to (d.3), (f), (g) and (j) and sections 112 and 113, in computing the taxpayer’s taxable income for the year, and

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 126(3)(b)(iii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an amount deducted under section 110.6 or paragraph 111(1)(b), or deductible under any of paragraphs 110(1)(d) to (d.3), (f), (g) and (j), in computing the taxpayer’s taxable income for the year,

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (3) apply to the 1997 and subsequent taxation years except that, for the 1997 taxation year

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subclause 126(1)(b)(ii)(A)(III) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), shall be read as follows:

Subclause.Label: (III)
Subclause.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an amount deducted by the taxpayer under section 110.6 or paragraph 111(1)(b), or deductible by the taxpayer under paragraph 110(1)(d), (d.1), (d.2), (d.3), (f), (g) or (j) or section 112 or 113, for the year or in respect of the period or periods referred to in subclause (II), as the case may be, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subclause 126(2.1)(a)(ii)(A)(III) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (2), shall be read as follows:

Subclause.Label: (III)
Subclause.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an amount deducted by the taxpayer under section 110.6 or paragraph 111(1)(b), or deductible by the taxpayer under paragraph 110(1)(d), (d.1), (d.2), (d.3), (f), (g) or (j) or section 112 or 113, for the year or in respect of the period or periods referred to in subclause (II), as the case may be, and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: subparagraph 126(3)(b)(iii) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (3), shall be read as follows:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an amount deducted under section 110.6 or paragraph 111(1)(b), or deductible under paragraph 110(1)(d), (d.1), (d.2), (d.3), (f), (g) or (j), in computing the individual’s taxable income for the year or in respect of the period or periods referred to in subparagraph (ii), as the case may be,

Subsection.Text: Notwithstanding subsections 152(4) to (5) of the Act, any assessment of a taxpayer’s tax, interest or penalty for any taxation year shall be made that is necessary to give effect to any of subsections (1) to (4)

Paragraph 127.52(1)(h) of the Act is amended by striking out the word “and” at the end of subparagraph (iv), by adding the word “and” at the end of subparagraph (v) and by adding the following after subparagraph (v):

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount deducted under paragraph 110(1)(g);

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 1997 and subsequent taxation years and, notwithstanding subsections 152(4) to (5) of the Act, any assessment of a taxpayer’s tax, interest or penalty for any taxation year shall be made that is necessary to give effect to subsection (1).
Paragraph 157(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the remainder of the taxes payable by it under this Part and Parts I.3, VI, VI.1 and XIII.1 for the year on or before its balance-due day for the year.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that end after 2001.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 157:

Section.MarginalNote: Instalment deferral for January, February and March 2002 — definitions

Subsection.Text: The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: eligible corporation, for a particular taxation year, means a corporation

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that is resident in Canada throughout the particular taxation year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: of which the taxable capital employed in Canada, within the meaning assigned by Part I.3, for its preceding taxation year did not exceed,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the corporation is not associated with any other corporation in the particular taxation year, $15 million, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the corporation is associated with one or more other corporations in the particular taxation year, the amount by which $15 million exceeds the total of the taxable capital employed in Canada, within the meaning assigned by Part I.3, of those other corporations for their last taxation years that ended in the last calendar year that ended before the end of the particular taxation year.

Definition.Text: eligible instalment day of an eligible corporation means a day in January, February or March, 2002, on which an instalment on account of the corporation’s tax payable under this Part for the taxation year that includes that day would become payable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if this Act were read without reference to this section; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if, in the case of a corporation that is not required by section 157 to make instalment payments on account of its tax payable under this Part for the taxation year, it were so required.

Subsection.Text: An eligible corporation’s balance-due day for a taxation year that ends after 2001 is deemed to be the later of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the day that would otherwise be the corporation’s balance-due day for the taxation year, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the day that is six months after the corporation’s last eligible instalment day in the taxation year.

Subsection.Text: An amount that would, because of paragraph 157(1)(a), otherwise become payable in respect of a taxation year by an eligible corporation on an eligible instalment day of the corporation does not become payable on that day but becomes payable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the particular day that is six months after the eligible instalment day is in the taxation year, on the particular day; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, on the day that is deemed by subsection (2) to be the corporation’s balance-due day for the taxation year.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that end after 2001.
Subsection 160.1(1.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Liability for refund by reason of s. 122.5
Subsection.Text: If a person is a qualified relation of an individual (within the meaning assigned by subsection 122.5(1)), in relation to one or more months specified for a taxation year, the person and the individual are jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable to pay the lesser of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any excess described in subsection (1) that was refunded in respect of the taxation year to, or applied to a liability of, the individual as a consequence of the operation of section 122.5, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the total of the amounts deemed by subsection 122.5(3) to have been paid by the individual during those specified months.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1.1) does not limit a person’s liability under any other provision of this Act.
Subsection (1) applies to amounts deemed to be paid during months specified for the 2001 and subsequent taxation years.
Paragraph (d) of the definition balance-due day in subsection 248(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: where the taxpayer is a corporation,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the day that is three months after the day on which the taxation year (in this subparagraph referred to as the “current year”) ends, if

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: an amount was deducted under section 125 in computing the corporation’s tax payable under this Part for the current year or for its preceding taxation year,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the corporation is, throughout the current year, a Canadian-controlled private corporation, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: either

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: in the case of a corporation that is not associated with another corporation in the current year, its taxable income for its preceding taxation year (determined before taking into consideration the specified future tax consequences for that preceding taxation year) does not exceed its business limit for that preceding taxation year, or

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: in the case of a corporation that is associated with one or more other corporations in the current year, the total of the taxable incomes of the corporation and of those other corporations for their last taxation years that ended in the last calendar year that ended before the end of the current year (determined before taking into consideration the specified future tax consequences for those last taxation years) does not exceed the total of the business limits of the corporation and of those other corporations for those last taxation years, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the day that is two months after the day on which the taxation year ends, in any other case;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that end after 2001.

Section.Text: The Canada Fund for Africa Act is enacted as follows:

BillInternal.LongTitle: An Act to establish a program to provide contributions for the economic and social development of Africa in fulfilment of the objectives set out in the New Partnership for Africa’s Development

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Canada Fund for Africa Act.
In this Act, Minister means the member of the Queen’s Privy Council for Canada who is designated by the Governor in Council as the Minister for the purposes of this Act.

Subsection.Text: There is hereby established a program, to be called the Canada Fund for Africa, the object of which is to provide for the payment of contributions to eligible recipients for the carrying out of eligible activities.

An eligible activity is an activity that is, or is to be, conducted by an eligible recipient and that, in the opinion of the Minister, significantly promotes the fulfilment of the objectives set out in the New Partnership for Africa’s Development, adopted as the New Africa Initiative by the Organization of African Unity at Lusaka in July 2001 and, in particular, those objectives that were identified for support in the Africa Action Plan called for by the Group of Eight industrialized countries in Genoa in July 2001 and that are adopted by the Group of Eight at its summit scheduled at Kananaskis in June 2002.

A foreign government, an agency of a foreign government, an international organization, a corporation, a partnership or a trust is an eligible recipient if it carries on or, in the opinion of the Minister, is capable of carrying on an eligible activity.

Section.Text: The Minister may enter into an agreement with an eligible recipient to provide for the payment of a contribution for an eligible activity under this Act.

The Governor in Council may make regulations generally for the carrying out of the purposes and provisions of this Act, including regulations establishing additional terms and conditions for the Fund.
The provisions enacted by section 45 come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
The Canada Strategic Infrastructure Fund Act is enacted as follows:

BillInternal.LongTitle: An Act to establish a program to provide contributions for the carrying out of strategic infrastructure projects

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Canada Strategic Infrastructure Fund Act.
The definitions in this section apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: eligible project means a large-scale project for the construction, renewal or material enhancement of strategic infrastructure that is carried out, or to be carried out, by an eligible recipient.
Minister means the member of the Queen’s Privy Council for Canada who is designated by the Governor in Council as the Minister for the purposes of this Act.
strategic infrastructure means any of the following fixed capital assets that are used or operated for the benefit of the public:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: highway or rail infrastructure;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: local transportation infrastructure;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: tourism or urban development infrastructure;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: sewage treatment infrastructure;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: water infrastructure; or

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: infrastructure prescribed by regulation.

Subsection.Text: There is hereby established a program, to be called the Canada Strategic Infrastructure Fund, the object of which is to provide for the payment of contributions to eligible recipients for the carrying out of large-scale strategic infrastructure projects that contribute to economic growth or quality of life in Canada and that advance Canada’s objectives with respect to infrastructure.
The Fund shall, where appropriate, promote the use of partnerships between public and private sector bodies.
The following are eligible recipients:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a province or a municipal or regional government established by or under provincial legislation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a public sector body that is established by or under provincial legislation or by regulation or is wholly owned by a province or a private sector body that is in partnership with a province or a government referred to in paragraph (a), if the body

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: carries out or, in the opinion of the Minister, is capable of carrying out an eligible project in Canada, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: has legal capacity, or is composed of organizations each of which has legal capacity; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a private sector body that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: carries out or, in the opinion of the Minister, is capable of carrying out an eligible project in Canada, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: has legal capacity, or is composed of organizations each of which has legal capacity.

Section.Text: The Minister may enter into an agreement with an eligible recipient to provide for the payment of a contribution for an eligible project under this Act.
The Governor in Council may make regulations generally for the carrying out of the purposes and provisions of this Act, including regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing other fixed capital assets for the purpose of paragraph (f) of the definition strategic infrastructure in section 2;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: defining what constitutes a large-scale project; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: establishing additional terms and conditions for the Fund.
